channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 3 ejercicios para prevenir el dolor de pies
item.description: La fascitis plantar puede ser increíblemente incómoda. Pero unos sencillos movimientos pueden aliviarla y prevenirla.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Loudin

media:content.media:credit: Nicholas Sansone para The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Pakistan’s Shuttle Diplomacy Unfolds in Tehran
item.description: The status of U.S.-Iran peace talks was not clear early Thursday, a day after a Pakistani delegation arrived in the Iranian capital. President Trump said Israeli and Lebanese leaders would speak later in the day.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: La creciente disputa sobre el papa e Irán aleja a Meloni de Trump
item.description: La primera ministra italiana fue considerada en su día una de las aliadas europeas más cercanas del presidente Trump. Su amistad parece ahora estar en peligro.
item.dc:creator: Jason Horowitz
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump saluda a la primera ministra de Italia, Giorgia Meloni, en la Casa Blanca el año pasado. En su día se consideraron estrechos aliados.

item.title: Elogio de los perros tontos
item.description: No pasa nada si tu mascota no es un genio.
item.dc:creator: Emily Anthes

media:content.media:credit: Peter Arkle

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for April 16, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Picture Prompt: Statue of Liberty
item.description: What do you think this image is communicating?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: punctual
item.description: This word has appeared in 19 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Stephen Colbert Wants the Vice President to Lay Off the Pope
item.description: The “Late Show” host scolded JD Vance for suggesting that Pope Leo XIV “be careful when he talks about matters of theology.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

item.title: Muchos migrantes en EE. UU. temen declarar impuestos, y eso podría causarle problemas al fisco
item.description: El temor a que el IRS comparta sus datos con el ICE ha convertido la temporada de la declaración de la renta en una apuesta arriesgada para muchos migrantes. El Laboratorio Presupuestario de Yale prevé una pérdida de unos 300.000 millones de dólares en una década.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan and Andrew Duehren

media:content.media:credit: Mark Abramson para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Muchos migrantes indocumentados que quieren hacer la declaración de la renta han buscado la ayuda de clínicas como esta de Los Ángeles.

item.title: I Almost Never Predict Supreme Court Outcomes. Trump Will Lose This Case.
item.description: This immigration case is really about procedure.
item.dc:creator: Linda Greenhouse

item.title: Drivers Stranded as Flooding Closes Major Highway in Milwaukee
item.description: Severe storms moving across the Midwest were bringing heavy rain and raising the risk of flash flooding, tornadoes and large hail.
item.dc:creator: Dan Watson and Erin McCann

media:content.media:credit: Mark Hoffman/Milwaukee Journal Sentinel
media:content.media:description: Emergency workers rescued drivers who were stranded by heavy rain that caused flooding in Milwaukee on Wednesday night.

item.title: 3 Killed in Boat Strike in the Pacific, Pentagon Says
item.description: It was the third such attack by the U.S. military in three days, and the 51st attack in a campaign against people who the United States has accused of smuggling drugs.
item.dc:creator: Francesca Regalado
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US Strikes on Alleged Drug Traffickers (2025- )

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Francis L. Donovan at a confirmation hearing in January to become the leader of U.S. Southern Command.

item.title: El bloqueo de EE. UU. frena el comercio de Irán
item.description: Más de una decena de buques de guerra de la Marina estadounidense están aplicando un bloqueo a los buques de todas las naciones que entren o salgan de zonas costeras o puertos iraníes.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: A vessel in the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday. Traffic is still at a fraction of its usual levels.

item.title: Corrections: April 16, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, April 16, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Amid War, U.S. Businesses Aim to Sustain Profit Streak
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, April 16, 2026.

item.title: Review: ‘The Fear of 13,’ With Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson, Doesn’t Add Up
item.description: Adrien Brody and Tessa Thompson make confident Broadway debuts, but the uneven script makes for a narratively slippery prison drama.
item.dc:creator: Helen Shaw

media:content.media:credit: Sara Krulwich/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Adrien Brody, left, and Ephraim Sykes in the Lindsey Ferrentino play “The Fear of 13” at the James Earl Jones Theater in Manhattan.

item.title: China’s G.D.P. Stronger Than Expected, Led by Infrastructure Spending
item.description: A steep slide in housing prices has left consumers less prosperous and less willing to spend, but the government is pouring money into new rail lines and other projects.
item.dc:creator: Keith Bradsher

media:content.media:credit: Jade Gao/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: An import and export trade show in southern China this week.

item.title: Lawyer John Eastman Disbarred for Efforts to Overturn 2020 Election
item.description: The California Supreme Court upheld a lower court’s decision that said Mr. Eastman, had violated the rules of professional ethics.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: John Eastman has defended his actions, and one of his lawyers said he would explore his options at the U.S. Supreme Court.

item.title: Iran Will Be in U.S. for World Cup, FIFA’s Infantino Says
item.description: The comments come as peace talks between Iran and the United States have not yet resumed and a cease-fire is set to expire next week.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Ahn

media:content.media:credit: Ali Haider/EPA, via Shutterstock
media:content.media:description: Iran tried unsuccessfully to move its matches from the United States to Mexico.

item.title: How Trump Plans to Make D.C.’s Triumphal Arch One of the World’s Largest
item.description: See how the 250-foot structure would dwarf other monumental arches, including the Arc de Triomphe in Paris.
item.dc:creator: Marco Hernandez and Anushka Patil

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for April 16, 2026
item.description: Rafael Musa and Sala Wanetick tag-team this tremendous Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Sean McGowan

media:content.media:credit: Justine Willard/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: We’re all cheering for you to solve this crossword.

item.title: Temperatures Hit 90 Along the East Coast
item.description: Remember those grimy snow piles that just wouldn’t melt? They’re gone now.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

media:content.media:credit: Rahmat Gul/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Hydration and shade were two key ways to stay cool on the National Mall in Washington, D.C., on Wednesday.

item.title: Senate Blocks Bid to Cancel Arms Sales to Israel as Iran War Deepens Democratic Divide
item.description: Concerns over the Iran war led several Democratic senators who had rejected past bids to curb weapons transfers to Israel to vote to block the sale of bulldozers and bombs.
item.dc:creator: Megan Mineiro
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Bernie Sanders, independent of Vermont, has long been critical of robust American aid for Israel’s military.

item.title: Sneaker Company Allbirds Plans to Pivot to A.I. Yes, A.I.
item.description: After selling its business for $39 million last month, the company said it planned to buy powerful computer chips and rebrand itself NewBird AI.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Jeenah Moon for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The shoe company Allbirds announced on Wednesday that it would “pivot its business” to artificial intelligence.

item.title: DeSantis Delays Redistricting Special Session and Adds a Vaccine Bill
item.description: The Florida governor is expected to propose a congressional map that could result in up to five new Republican-leaning seats. Some in the party fear it could backfire.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and Pooja Salhotra

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida, a Republican, middle, said he would delay a special session on redistricting by a week.

item.title: Analysis of Alzheimer’s Drugs Stirs Debate About Their Effectiveness
item.description: The review said a certain class of drugs had little clinical benefit, but many Alzheimer’s experts criticized the analysis, saying it unfairly lumped failed drugs with two recently approved treatments.
item.dc:creator: Pam Belluck

media:content.media:credit: Heather Khalifa/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A clinical trial participant receiving an infusion of an anti-amyloid Alzheimer’s drug in New York last year.

item.title: How Trump’s Clash With Pope Leo Turned Into a Fight Over Theology
item.description: When President Trump and G.O.P. leaders denounced the pope’s comments about the U.S. attack on Iran, they touched off a religious debate over what is and isn’t a “just war.”
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias

media:content.media:credit: Guglielmo Mangiapane/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV at the Basilica of Our Lady of Africa in Algiers on Monday.

item.title: Mamdani’s Wife Admits ‘Shame’ Over Social Media Posts From Her Teens
item.description: Rama Duwaji apologized for using what she said was “harmful” language as a teenager, in her first interview since her husband, Zohran Mamdani, took office.
item.dc:creator: Katherine Rosman

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Rama Duwaji, the wife of Mayor Zohran Mamdani, said she was “truly sorry” for what she acknowledged was harmful language that she used as a teenager in social media posts.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the Live Nation Antitrust Trial
item.description: A jury found that the concert giant operated as a monopoly, a verdict that could have major reverberations in the music industry.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario

media:content.media:credit: Mike Blake/Reuters
media:content.media:description: A judge will now determine damages and remedies in the case, which could involve divestments or a breakup of the company.

item.title: What Is the Pied-à-Terre Tax? 5 Things to Know About Hochul’s Proposal.
item.description: Gov. Kathy Hochul wants to tax second homes in New York City that are worth $5 million or more. Here’s how the proposal might work.
item.dc:creator: Ronda Kaysen
item.category: Real Estate Board of New York

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Schultz for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Kathy Hochul’s proposal on taxing second homes would address the city budget gap by targeting the very wealthy.

item.title: Shakespeare Bought One Property in London. Now We Know Exactly Where.
item.description: In confirming the precise location of William Shakespeare’s Blackfriars house, a British scholar raises fresh questions about what he intended to do with it.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle

media:content.media:credit: Jeremie Souteyrat for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: William Shakespeare’s property was just across the river from the Globe Theater, left, which staged his plays then and does so today, having been recreated and reopened in 1997.

item.title: Laura Dogu, principal enviada de EE. UU a Venezuela, deja el cargo
item.description: Laura Dogu, quien reabrió la embajada de Estados Unidos en Venezuela, vuelve a su rol como asesora del oficial de mayor rango del ejército estadounidense mientras continúa la guerra con Irán.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero

media:content.media:credit: Leonardo Fernandez Viloria/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Laura Dogu, la principal diplomática estadounidense en Venezuela, el mes pasado en Caracas.

item.title: Videos Show Officers Beating Man During Arrest in Brooklyn
item.description: Two officers were placed under investigation after the videos surfaced. Mayor Zohran Mamdani called them disturbing. The Police Department said officers incorrectly identified the man.
item.dc:creator: Maia Coleman

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Police Commissioner Jessica Tisch said the videos that appeared to show officers beating a man were “deeply disturbing.”

item.title: Carol Greitzer, Tireless Defender of Greenwich Village, Dies at 101
item.description: A lifelong New Yorker and longtime City Council member, she opposed Robert Moses’s wrecking ball, championed historic preservation and fought for women’s and gay rights.
item.dc:creator: Clay Risen

media:content.media:credit: William E. Sauro/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ms. Greitzer in 1985 at a news conference with Mr. Koch. By then, she was a longtime member of the City Council and he was more than halfway through his term as mayor.

item.title: Sudan Enters Fourth Year of War Amid World’s Most Severe Humanitarian Crisis
item.description: Fighting that erupted in 2023 has forced millions from their homes and left widespread hunger that is now being compounded by strife in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni and Declan Walsh

media:content.media:credit: Bernat Armangue/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: A 2-month-old patient, Saidal Altaher, getting treatment for malnutrition on Wednesday in Port Sudan, Sudan.

item.title: Trump’s Portrayal of the War in Iran Collides With Reality
item.description: President Trump is confronting a crisis that is not bending to his narrative of a “pretty reasonable” new regime in Iran and all-but-assured victory for the United States.
item.dc:creator: Anton Troianovski
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump in Miami last week. Mr. Trump has declared a “regime change” accomplishment in Iran, even though analysts believe the war may have only increased the internal sway of Iran’s Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps.

item.title: El gobierno de Brasil enfrenta una controversia laboral con BYD
item.description: La salida del principal inspector de trabajo de Brasil ha puesto al gobierno del presidente Lula, político de izquierda y exdirigente sindical, en una situación incómoda.
item.dc:creator: Ana Ionova
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:credit: Victor Moriyama para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Una fábrica de BYD en construcción el año pasado en Camaçari, Bahía, Brasil. Una inspección del Ministerio de Trabajo descubrió que los trabajadores chinos que construían la planta vivían en condiciones miserables.

item.title: 2 Men Plead Not Guilty to Supporting ISIS in Gracie Mansion Attack
item.description: The defendants, Emir Balat, 18, and Ibrahim Kayumi, 19, face federal charges in the homemade-bomb attack on an anti-Muslim protest in March. No one was injured.
item.dc:creator: Wesley Parnell
item.category: Islamic State in Iraq and Syria (ISIS)

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Prosecutors say that two teenagers from Pennsylvania, inspired by ISIS, drove to Manhattan in March with the intent to kill people at a protest using homemade explosive devices.

item.title: Justice Sotomayor Apologizes for Highly Personal Criticism of Justice Kavanaugh
item.description: At the University of Kansas School of Law last week, she criticized her colleague while discussing his views in an immigration-related case.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Justice Sonia Sotomayor in Washington last year. In response to a question last week she suggested that Justice Brett M. Kavanaugh was out of touch with the experiences of working-class people.

item.title: The Pied-à-Terre Tax Has Failed Before. Could This Year Be Different?
item.description: Amid calls from the left to tax the rich, a tax proposal on multimillion-dollar second homes in New York City, backed by Gov. Kathy Hochul, seems to have better odds of passing than in years past.
item.dc:creator: Grace Ashford and Jeffery C. Mays

media:content.media:credit: Karsten Moran for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The proposed tax on second homes worth more than $5 million would yield “at least $500 million” in recurring revenue, the Hochul administration said.

item.title: Stocks Hit a Record High
item.description: Also, Pakistani mediators arrive in Iran. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

item.title: Trump’s ‘Triumphal Arch’ Draws Backlash, Even From an Expert Who Proposed It
item.description: The story of how President Trump quadrupled the size of the original proposal for the arch follows a now-familiar pattern.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater and Zachary Small

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, showed the latest renderings for a proposed triumphal arch during a briefing on Wednesday.

item.title: Can Trump and Republicans Get Back on Message on the Economy?
item.description: It’s Tax Day, but much of the country’s focus has been on an unpopular war.
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson

item.title: In the House, Republican Plans Go Awry Amid Party Divides
item.description: Fresh off a two-week break, lawmakers returned to turmoil in the House, where legislation to reopen the Department of Homeland Security is stalled and the G.O.P. is struggling to keep its agenda on track.
item.dc:creator: Michael Gold

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Speaker Mike Johnson on the steps of the Capitol on Wednesday.

item.title: U.K.’s Starmer Summons U.S. Social Media Companies Over Child Safety Online
item.description: The British prime minister will meet with executives from Meta, Google and other companies on Thursday as his government explores how to protect children from online harms.
item.dc:creator: Michael D. Shear

media:content.media:credit: Pool photo by Stefan Rousseau
media:content.media:description: Prime Minister Keir Starmer of Britain speaking about online safety at a school in London last month.

item.title: The Pope in Africa
item.description: The continent is crucial to the future of the church, but it’s also the center of long-running tensions over some of Catholicism’s most charged issues.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Beloumou Olomo/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV arrived in Cameroon’s capital, Yaoundé, yesterday.

item.title: One-Pot White Wine Pasta
item.description: While this pasta is reminiscent of scampi, piccata and vongole, it delivers something more — a hard-to-pin-down complexity that comes from cooking the pasta not in water but in wine.

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Testani for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Monica Pierini.

item.title: Spaghetti all’Assassina
item.description: This spicy one-pot pasta dish is common on menus in Bari, Italy, but can easily be prepared at home.

media:content.media:credit: Bobbi Lin for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Eugene Jho.

item.title: Coconut-Miso Salmon Curry
item.description: This light, delicate weeknight curry comes together in less than 30 minutes and is defined by its deep miso flavor.

media:content.media:credit: Julia Gartland for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Liza Jernow.

item.title: Pakistani Mediators Arrive in Iran to Keep Peace Talks Alive
item.description: The diplomacy came as the White House dismissed reports that President Trump wanted to extend the cease-fire, while still expressing optimism that talks could yield an agreement.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
